accurate.why: Accurately describes both the prior law and the change; the description of payment rules under the new law is correct.

no_hallucinations.why: The description of 'current law' states 'the first leaseholder to drill must pay any royalties due to Interior for all oil and gas production on the land' — this characterization of prior law is not found in the bill text and may be an oversimplification; however, it is a description of prior law context, not the amended text, and is not contradicted by the bill. Marginal pass issue; no clear hallucination.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the purpose is modifying the royalty payment process for joint drilling agreement leaseholders.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Explicitly contrasts the current law with the new law, correctly describing the change.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the Indian lands exclusion and the interest waiver condition.

effective_dates.why: No specific effective date mentioned; acceptable for a summary.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers the 120-day deadline, payment per proposed allocation, correction window, and interest waiver.

coherent.why: Well-organized with clear contrast of old vs. new law.

correct_entities.why: Federal Oil and Gas Royalty Management Act of 1982 and Department of the Interior correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: 120-day deadline and third-month correction window are accurate.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
